edges.description: Many confirmed DNETs carry FGFR1 alterations that activate MAPK signaling.
The cortical glioneuronal lesion is the structural substrate for focal seizures.
MAPK-driven glioneuronal tumor formation creates an epileptogenic cortical lesion.

nodes.description: Anti-seizure medications are used to manage DNET-associated epilepsy, although seizures are often drug-resistant before definitive surgical management.

evidence.reference_title: Review of seizure outcomes after surgical resection of dysembryoplastic neuroepithelial tumors.
evidence.snippet: The number of seizure-free patients who discontinued anti-epileptic drugs varied widely from zero to all patients.

nodes.description: DNET forms as a benign cortical glioneuronal tumor with neuronal and glial components, often involving temporal or frontal cortex and producing an epilepsy-associated lesion rather than a high-grade infiltrative glioma.

evidence.reference_title: Clinicopathological features of dysembryoplastic neuroepithelial tumor: a case series.
evidence.snippet: RESULTS: Fourteen cases were reported. There were 12 males and 2 females. Age range was 9-45 years (mean 19 years). Majority were located in the temporal and frontal lobes.

nodes.description: Specific DNET is molecularly more homogeneous than historical non-specific or diffuse DNT diagnoses. Broader DNET/MNGT/PLNTY-spectrum tumors can harbor BRAF, FGFR1, NF1, PDGFRA, FGFR2, NTRK2, and other alterations that converge on MAPK and PI3K/mTOR signaling, so molecular diagnosis helps separate true DNET from overlapping low-grade neuroepithelial entities.

evidence.reference_title: The genomic landscape of dysembryoplastic neuroepithelial tumours and a comprehensive analysis of recurrent cases.
evidence.snippet: Specific DNT is a homogeneous group of tumours sharing characteristics of paediatric low-grade gliomas: a quiet genome with a recurrent genomic alteration in the RAS-MAPK signalling pathway, a distinct DNA methylation profile and a good prognosis but showing progression in some cases.

evidence.reference_title: Genomic Analysis of Dysembryoplastic Neuroepithelial Tumor Spectrum Reveals a Diversity of Molecular Alterations Dysregulating the MAPK and PI3K/mTOR Pathways.
evidence.snippet: Overall, DNT-MNGT spectrum tumors exhibit diverse genomic alterations, with more than half (19/33) leading to MAPK/PI3K pathway alterations.

meta.association: Common somatic driver, with rare familial germline predisposition reported.

evidence.reference_title: Germline and somatic FGFR1 abnormalities in dysembryoplastic neuroepithelial tumors.
evidence.snippet: By whole-exome sequencing of the familial cases, we identified a novel germline FGFR1 mutation, p.R661P.

evidence.reference_title: Germline and somatic FGFR1 abnormalities in dysembryoplastic neuroepithelial tumors.
evidence.snippet: Somatic activating FGFR1 mutations (p.N546K or p.K656E) were observed in the tumor samples

evidence.reference_title: Germline and somatic FGFR1 abnormalities in dysembryoplastic neuroepithelial tumors.
evidence.snippet: further evidence for functional relevance was obtained by in silico modeling.

nodes.description: Specific DNETs are enriched for somatic FGFR1 alterations, including hotspot mutations, tyrosine kinase-domain duplications, and rare fusions/breakpoints. These events converge on RAS-MAPK signaling and provide the central molecular mechanism for many neuropathology-confirmed tumors.

evidence.reference_title: Germline and somatic FGFR1 abnormalities in dysembryoplastic neuroepithelial tumors.
evidence.snippet: In 43 sporadic cases, in which the diagnosis of DNET could be confirmed on central blinded neuropathology review, FGFR1 alterations were also frequent and mainly comprised intragenic tyrosine kinase FGFR1 duplication and multiple mutants in cis (25/43; 58.1 %) while BRAF p.V600E alterations were absent (0/43).

evidence.reference_title: Germline and somatic FGFR1 abnormalities in dysembryoplastic neuroepithelial tumors.
evidence.snippet: In conclusion, constitutional and somatic FGFR1 alterations and MAP kinase pathway activation are key events in the pathogenesis of DNET.

nodes.description: Long-standing focal seizures are the hallmark clinical presentation and may be drug-resistant before surgery.

evidence.reference_title: Clinicopathological features of dysembryoplastic neuroepithelial tumor: a case series.
evidence.snippet: BACKGROUND: Dysembryoplastic neuroepithelial tumors are rare benign supratentotrial epilepsy-associated glioneuronal tumors of children and young adults. Patients have a long history of seizures.

evidence.reference_title: Germline and somatic FGFR1 abnormalities in dysembryoplastic neuroepithelial tumors.
evidence.snippet: Dysembryoplastic neuroepithelial tumor (DNET) is a benign brain tumor associated with intractable drug-resistant epilepsy.

nodes.description: Mirdametinib is an investigational brain-penetrant MEK1/2 inhibitor being evaluated in the SJ901 pediatric/AYA low-grade glioma trial; for DNET, this is mechanistically relevant to FGFR1-driven RAS-MAPK activation but remains investigational rather than established standard care.

evidence.reference_title: SJ901: Phase 1/2 Evaluation of Single Agent Mirdametinib (PD-0325901), a Brain-Penetrant MEK1/2 Inhibitor, for the Treatment of Children, Adolescents, and Young Adults With Low-Grade Glioma
evidence.snippet: This is an open-label, multi-center, Phase 1/2 study of the brain-penetrant MEK inhibitor, mirdametinib (PD-0325901), in patients with pediatric low-grade glioma (pLGG).

nodes.description: Maximal safe resection or lesionectomy is the main disease-directed therapy, aiming for tumor control and seizure freedom. Shorter epilepsy duration and gross total removal predict better seizure outcomes.

evidence.reference_title: Long-Term Seizure Outcomes and Predictors in Patients with Dysembryoplastic Neuroepithelial Tumors Associated with Epilepsy.
evidence.snippet: At the final follow-up, 49 patients (77.8%) were seizure-free.

evidence.reference_title: Long-Term Seizure Outcomes and Predictors in Patients with Dysembryoplastic Neuroepithelial Tumors Associated with Epilepsy.
evidence.snippet: Gross total removal of the tumor and a short epilepsy duration were significant predictors of seizure freedom.

evidence.reference_title: Review of seizure outcomes after surgical resection of dysembryoplastic neuroepithelial tumors.
evidence.snippet: The median seizure freedom rate was 86% (interquartile range 77-93%) with only one study reporting fewer than 60% of patients seizure free.

nodes.description: DNET is an epileptogenic cortical tumor. Seizures arise when the glioneuronal lesion involves cortical neurons and related networks; peritumoral epileptogenic zones and associated focal cortical dysplasia can contribute to drug-resistant focal epilepsy.

evidence.reference_title: Low-grade epilepsy-associated neuroepithelial tumors: Tumor spectrum and diagnosis based on genetic alterations.
evidence.snippet: Brain tumors can always result in seizures when involving the cortical neurons or their circuits, and they were found to be one of the most common etiologies of intractable focal seizures.
